summary: Removed password authentication from owner login. The owner now enters only their email; the backend self-heals the Supabase password using the configured IVX_OWNER_PASSWORD and returns a real Supabase session, which the app installs via setSession(). Chat approval works automatically because it is a genuine Supabase session.

backend.new: backend/api/ivx-owner-passwordless-login.ts — POST /api/ivx/owner-passwordless-login (email-only, self-healing password grant, never returns the password)

frontend.authContext: expo/lib/auth-context.tsx — added loginOwnerPasswordless(email) method: calls the backend, installs the returned session via supabase.auth.setSession(), runs the existing 2FA + handleSession gate
frontend.loginScreen: expo/app/login.tsx — owner mode now hides the password field; the Sign In button calls handleOwnerPasswordlessLogin; the old server-password-repair card is removed from owner mode. A fallback 'Sign in with email + password' link remains for accounts that already have a password.

whyThisFixesTheRecurringIssue: The owner was repeatedly blocked by Supabase 'invalid_credentials' because the password typed on the phone did not match the password stored in Supabase.

Passwordless flow removes the password from the phone entirely. The backend uses IVX_OWNER_PASSWORD (a server-side env secret) and self-heals it on every login: it resets the Supabase user's password to that value before performing the password grant.
The owner never sees or types a password, so a password mismatch can never block them again.

Because the backend returns real Supabase session tokens (access_token + refresh_token) and the app installs them via setSession(), the owner-AI chat approval path (owner allowlist + bearer check) works unchanged.
